# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: web-server
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web
  port:
    number: 8080
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1.1

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: web-to-api-auth
  namespace: default
spec:
  server:
    name: web-server
  client:
    meshTLS:
      serviceAccounts:
      - name: api
        namespace: default

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: api-server
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: api
  port:
    number: 9090
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/2

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: api-to-db-auth
  namespace: default
spec:
  server:
    name: api-server
  client:
    meshTLS:
      serviceAccounts:
      - name: database
        namespace: default

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: db-server
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: database
  port:
    number: 3306
  proxyProtocol: TCP

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: db-to-none-auth
  namespace: default
spec:
  server:
    name: db-server
  client:
    meshTLS:
      serviceAccounts:
      - name: none
        namespace: default
